body.title: Add target_difficulty field to ticket schema (1-5 Apprentice→Teacher)
body.result: DIFFICULTY_TIERS constant + target_difficulty field (1-5 Apprentice→Teacher): default=1, validates on add, displays tier in list for diff>1. 10 tests. Commits ec9e32de (tests) + 85af3f7 (schema).
body.description:
Tickets need a capability tier so consumers can self-select work they can actually complete. Add `target_difficulty: int` (1-5) to cc_queue.py ticket schema. Tier names: Apprentice(1/Igor), Sustainer(2/Librarian), Creator(3/Haiku), Master(4/Sonnet), Teacher(5/Opus). Existing tickets with no field default to 1 (backward-compat). Validate 1-5 range on add; reject out-of-range with error. Display tier name alongside difficulty in `list` and `show` output.

**Affected files:** ~/TheIgors/lab/claudecode/cc_queue.py
**Design rules:** backward-compat default=1; validate at cmd_add; no-sqlite
**Scope boundary:** Schema field + display only. No filtering logic (that's T-queue-next-difficulty). No consumer changes.
**Test plan:** Adding ticket with no difficulty defaults to 1; adding with difficulty=3 stores 3 and displays 'Creator(3)'; invalid difficulty (0, 6, 'abc') rejected with clear error; existing queue.json tickets without the field still load correctly.
